required_operator_inputs: keep the mapped advanced-color inventory fixed and make the next backend-recovery slice truthful by extracting the shipped Source/Palette tuple and param authority out of color_pipeline_window.h
preserve shipped single-row Source/Palette compatibility while moving the exact shipped tuple and param seams into reusable core/runtime helpers
follow strict TDD: add the focused RED first, then land the minimal shipped-seam authority extraction
do not start Grading implementation or invent unsupported multi-row Source/Palette semantics before the remaining Phase 5 backend seams close honestly
forbidden_operator_prompts: claim concrete multi-row Source/Palette execution support without a checked-in plan that names the first supported recipe family
leave shipped Source/Palette tuple or param truth in color_pipeline_window.h after claiming the extraction slice landed
redefine the mapped advanced-color inventory or reopen MVP scope
widen Grading, Blend, Mask/Domain, or the common-fractal queue in the same slice

required_acceptance_assertions.description: The advanced color library foundation Phase 5 Source/Palette runtime-authority contract validates successfully
The advanced color foundation and Source/Palette recovery plans stay synchronized during the slice
